mutations.description: Accept a reserved I2C address below the permitted seven-bit range.
Accept a reserved I2C address above the permitted seven-bit range.
Start an I2C transaction without any payload bytes.
Permit a write longer than the bounded fixture payload.
Allow a new transaction before a terminal result is consumed.
Issue START without clearing stale status bits first.
Fail to assert the START control bit for a new transaction.
Write the seven-bit address without its write-direction bit position.
Fail to advance from the first byte after address acknowledgement.
Report successful completion without releasing the I2C bus.
Drive STOP even though this controller has lost ownership of the bus.
Ignore bus error priority when bus error and NACK are reported together.
Time out only after rather than at the configured deadline.
Clear timeout state without asking the peripheral to release the bus.
Leave latched status bits behind after the timeout abort.
